name: Follow Up on a Referral Without Burning the Referrer
description: Call a prospect who was introduced via a mutual contact, using the referrer's name responsibly so the lead converts AND the referrer's relationship with both parties is preserved or strengthened.
disclaimer: Skills assume the call is solicited by the operator. NEVER use these to power a mass-cold-call campaign without the operator's explicit per-prospect authorisation.

context.when_to_use: Operator has been introduced to a prospect by a mutual contact — either via a double-opt-in email intro, a verbal 'you should talk to them', or a public mention. Agent is making the follow-up call.
preconditions: The referrer has explicitly given permission to be named, OR has made a public introduction (e.g. CC'd intro email).
The operator has briefed the agent on the referrer relationship (close friend? Casual acquaintance? Past colleague?).
The agent knows what the referrer told the prospect about the operator.
The agent has a clear, short reason for the call that matches what the referrer set up.

principles: The referrer's reputation is collateral on this call. Behave accordingly.
Match the warmth of the introduction. A casual 'you two should talk' is NOT the same as a written endorsement — don't oversell the closeness.
Land the conversation with low pressure. Aggressive selling on a referred call damages the referrer more than a cold call ever could.
Update the referrer afterwards — briefly. Silence after they did you a favour is the fastest way to lose future referrals.
If the call uncovers that the prospect isn't a fit, say so. Forcing it makes the referrer regret introducing you.

phrases.opener_email_intro: Hi [target first name], it's [agent name] from [operator]'s side — following up on the intro [referrer first name] sent across last week.
phrases.opener_verbal: Hi [target first name], this is [agent name] on behalf of [operator]. [Referrer name] mentioned we should connect — they thought we might be useful to each other on [topic].
phrases.calibrate_warmth: Just so I'm not assuming — how do you know [referrer]? I want to make sure I'm bringing the right context.
phrases.low_pressure_ask: [Referrer] suggested a quick conversation rather than email. I'm not here to pitch you — I'd love 10 minutes to understand what you're working on and then we can decide together if there's anything worth doing next.
phrases.redirect_if_misfit: Honestly, from what you're describing, I'm not sure we're the most useful — but I'd like to be straight about that rather than waste your time. Want me to tell [referrer] we talked and it wasn't quite the right shape?
phrases.graceful_close: This was useful — thanks for the time. I'll send [referrer] a quick thanks and let them know we connected. I'll follow up with you on [specific next step] by [day].

tactics.name: Lead with the referrer's name, not the operator's company
tactics.script: Use `opener_email_intro` or `opener_verbal`. The referrer's name is the credential — front-load it.

tactics.name: Calibrate the relationship the referrer has with the target
tactics.script: Use `calibrate_warmth`. A 'we worked together 10 years ago' is different from 'best friends' — your tone and the depth of the ask should follow that signal.

tactics.name: Explicitly de-pressurise
tactics.script: Use `low_pressure_ask`. State out loud that this is not a pitch. Referred calls that turn into pitches get reported back to the referrer fast.

tactics.name: Reflect the referrer's language
tactics.when: Whenever discussing why this call is happening.
tactics.script: If you have the referrer's exact phrasing ('they're working on something similar to what you did at X'), reuse it. The target will recognise it and relax.

tactics.name: Volunteer disqualification before pushing
tactics.when: First sign that the fit isn't right.
tactics.script: Use `redirect_if_misfit`. Walking away protects the referrer's standing and often leads to a counter-referral from the target.

tactics.name: Close the loop with the referrer within 24 hours
tactics.when: After the call ends, regardless of outcome.
tactics.script: Send a 3-line note to the referrer: thank, outcome (next step / not a fit), what you learned. Do this even — especially — when the fit was wrong.

boundaries: Do NOT exaggerate the closeness of the referrer relationship beyond what is true.
Do NOT name the referrer in voicemails, on hold music, or to assistants without permission to do so.
Do NOT use a referral as cover for a hard sell — the prospect will tell the referrer, and you've lost two people.
Do NOT promise the prospect things the referrer mentioned the operator could do unless the operator has confirmed.
Do NOT share any confidential context the referrer gave you about the prospect.
Do NOT contact the prospect more than once via phone if they don't respond — the referrer's name does not unlock additional attempts.
success_signals: Prospect references the referrer warmly ('oh yeah, [referrer] told me about you').
Prospect engages substantively on the topic the intro framed.
A concrete next step is agreed on the call.
Prospect asks for a way to reciprocate or makes their own offer.
failure_signals: Prospect doesn't remember the referrer or seems unclear why the call is happening.
Prospect was 'just being polite' to the referrer and isn't actually interested.
Prospect mentions a misalignment that the referrer should have known about.
Prospect declines a next step without a specific reason.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm the agreed next step, send the recap email, and CC the referrer ONLY if the referrer asked to stay looped in (otherwise send a separate thank-you).
exit_strategy.on_failure: Close the call warmly. Send the referrer a short, honest note. Do not retry the prospect by phone without re-engaging the referrer first.
follow_ups: Email to the referrer within 24 hours: 'Thanks again, here's what happened.'
Calendar entry for any agreed next-step date with the prospect.
CRM note tagging this as a referral, with the referrer's name and the closeness rating.

required_user_info: Referrer's name, relationship to the operator, and explicit permission to use their name
How the introduction happened (email / verbal / public mention)
What the referrer told the prospect about the operator
Whether the referrer wants to be CC'd on follow-ups
